findings.explanation: Interprocedural leak: `make_buffer()` returns the allocation; the caller (`main`) drops `buf` without calling free(). The allocation at <WORKSPACE>/demo/memory_leak_corpus/simple_leak/main.c:5 (`buf`) has no matching free on the leaking path.
findings.repair_suggestion: Ensure the object allocated via malloc in make_buffer is released on every exit path. Add cleanup before each return or route ownership to a caller that clearly frees it.

root_cause.description: the function transfers ownership by returning the allocation, but the caller never frees it
root_cause.rootCauseDescription: `make_buffer()` returns the allocation; the caller (`main`) drops `buf` without calling free().

repair_diff.description: Free `buf` before the exit at line 20 so every path releases the allocation.
